---
title: "Pre-Hackathon Setup"
subtitle: "Get your tools ready. Tomorrow, you build."
author: "Jayrup Nakawala"
format: 
  revealjs:
    theme: night
    transition: slide
    slide-number: true
    preview-links: auto
    chalkboard: true
    code-line-numbers: false
  html:
    toc: true
    theme: darkly
execute:
  cache: true
---

# What We're Doing Today

## The Goal

By the end of this session, your machine will be ready for the hackathon.

:::{.fragment}
1. **KiloCode** installed and connected to a free AI model
2. **Git** installed and working
3. **GitHub** account logged in locally
:::

:::{.fragment}
If you leave today without all three, you'll waste time tomorrow.
:::

# Step 1: KiloCode

## What is KiloCode?

An AI coding agent that lives **inside VSCode**.

- It reads your files
- It writes code
- It runs terminal commands
- It fixes its own errors

:::{.fragment}
All without leaving your editor.
:::

## Install KiloCode

1. Open VSCode
2. Go to Extensions (Ctrl+Shift+X)
3. Search **KiloCode**
4. Click Install

:::{.fragment}
Do this now. I'll wait.
:::

## Set Up a Free AI Model

KiloCode needs an AI provider. We'll use **NVIDIA** — it's free.

1. Go to `build.nvidia.com`
2. Create an account (or sign in)
3. Get your **API key**

:::{.callout-tip}
You don't need to pay for anything. NVIDIA gives free access to models on this platform.
:::

## Connect KiloCode to NVIDIA

1. Open KiloCode settings in VSCode
2. Select **NVIDIA** as your provider
3. Paste your API key
4. Pick a model

:::{.fragment}
Done. You now have a free AI coding assistant.
:::

## Test It

Open the KiloCode panel and type:

> "Hello, can you create a file called test.txt with the text 'it works'?"

:::{.fragment}
If a file appeared, you're set. Delete it and move on.
:::

:::{.fragment}
If it didn't work, raise your hand.
:::

# Step 2: Git

## What is Git?

Git tracks changes to your code.

:::{.fragment}
Your hackathon project will break. Multiple times. Git lets you go back to when it worked.
:::

## Install Git

Check if you already have it:

```bash
git --version
```

:::{.fragment}
If it says "command not found":

- **Windows:** Download from `git-scm.com`
- **Mac:** Run `xcode-select --install`
- **Linux:** `sudo apt install git` or `sudo pacman -S git`
:::

## Configure Git

Tell Git who you are:

```bash
git config --global user.name "Your Name"
git config --global user.email "your.email@example.com"
```

:::{.callout-tip}
Use the same email as your GitHub account.
:::

## The Basics

```bash
git init                          # start tracking
git add .                         # stage everything
git commit -m "describe what you did"  # save snapshot
```

:::{.callout-tip}
Commit often. After every working feature. Not once at the end.
:::

## When Things Break

```bash
git restore .                     # undo all changes since last commit
git checkout [hash]               # go back to a specific commit
```

:::{.fragment}
This is your undo button. Use it aggressively.
:::

:::{.callout-tip}
You don't have to memorise any of this. KiloCode can run git commands for you — just ask it to "commit my changes" or "push to GitHub".
:::

# Step 3: GitHub

## Why GitHub?

- Your team works on different laptops
- You need to share code
- You need a backup in the cloud

:::{.fragment}
If your laptop dies, your project lives on. That's the point.
:::

## Create a GitHub Account

If you don't have one:

1. Go to `github.com`
2. Sign up (free)

:::{.fragment}
Do this now if you haven't already.
:::

## Log In Locally

Your machine needs to authenticate with GitHub.

The easiest way:

```bash
gh auth login
```

:::{.fragment}
Don't have `gh`? Install the GitHub CLI:

- **Windows:** `winget install GitHub.cli`
- **Mac:** `brew install gh`
- **Linux:** `sudo apt install gh`
:::

## Alternative: HTTPS Authentication

If you prefer not to install `gh`:

```bash
git push origin main
```

Git will prompt you for your GitHub username and a **Personal Access Token** (not your password).

:::{.callout-tip}
Get a token: GitHub → Settings → Developer Settings → Personal Access Tokens → Generate New Token. Give it `repo` scope.
:::

## Test It

Create a test repo on GitHub, then:

```bash
mkdir test-project && cd test-project
git init
echo "# Test" > README.md
git add . && git commit -m "first commit"
git remote add origin https://github.com/YOU/test-project.git
git push -u origin main
```

:::{.fragment}
If it pushed, you're logged in. Delete the repo later if you want.
:::

## Collaboration Workflow

Tomorrow, your team will need this:

```bash
git pull                          # get latest changes
# ... do your work ...
git add .
git commit -m "add login feature"
git push                          # upload your changes
```

:::{.callout-warning}
Always `git pull` before you start working. Merge conflicts are avoidable.
:::

## Merge Conflicts

Two people edited the same line? Git will ask you to choose.

```
<<<<<<< HEAD
your code
=======
their code
>>>>>>> branch-name
```

:::{.fragment}
Pick one. Delete the markers. Commit. Move on with your life.
:::

# Checklist

## Before You Leave

Make sure all of these work:

- [ ] VSCode is installed
- [ ] KiloCode extension is installed
- [ ] NVIDIA account created, API key connected
- [ ] KiloCode responds to prompts
- [ ] `git --version` works
- [ ] `git config` is set (name + email)
- [ ] GitHub account exists
- [ ] You can `git push` to GitHub

:::{.fragment}
If anything is broken, fix it now. Not tomorrow morning.
:::

## Questions? {.center}

## See you at the hackathon.{.center}
